question: When applying equivalence partitioning to test a field that accepts integer values between 1 and 100 inclusive, which of the following BEST represents the complete set of partitions?
options: One valid partition (1–100) and one invalid partition (all other values)
One valid partition (1–100), one invalid partition below (≤ 0), and one invalid partition above (≥ 101)
Three valid partitions: low (1–33), mid (34–66), and high (67–100)
One valid partition (1–100) only, since invalid inputs need not be tested
explanation: TA - Equivalence partitioning divides input data into partitions where the software is expected to behave the same way. For a range of 1–100 there is one valid partition and two invalid partitions: values below the minimum (≤ 0) and values above the maximum (≥ 101). Testing one representative value from each partition is sufficient.
